title: kbu-start v1 — student-facing notebook workflow (two-tier slash command + state-machine + harvested BERIL patterns)
author: Chris Henry + /ai-design
problem: KBUtilLib has no student-facing front door. Existing skills (kbutillib-expert, kbutillib-dev) assume expertise. Students lack workflow, layout, environment setup, and a way to use Claude Code productively for notebook design. Must also work for users outside Chris's environment (no ClaudeCommands, no AIAssistant).
solution: Two-tier /kbu-start slash command shipped inside KBUtilLib's own .claude/ (policy exception for distribution). Tier-1 (KBUtilLib repo): Help / Initialize / New project / Update. Tier-2 (student projects copied from templates/student-project/): Plan / Build / Run / Synthesize / Review / Literature review / Diagnose / Update, gated on a linear state machine (plan → p-review → build → b-review → run → synthesize → s-review → complete). State, sessions, notebook tracking, AIAssistant routing, venv management all behind the kbu CLI. Sessions auto-route to AIAssistant sessions.db if present, else local YAML in subproject.

decisions.distribution_policy: .claude/ is checked in to KBUtilLib; claude-skills sync does NOT manage these skills. Tier-2 template at templates/student-project/ copied verbatim by kbu new-project. Pre-existing kbutillib-dev / kbutillib-expert unaffected.
decisions.tier_split: Two same-named files: tier-1 .claude/commands/kbu-start.md (4 items) and tier-2 templates/student-project/.claude/commands/kbu-start.md (8 items). Each focused; no polymorphic context detection.
decisions.state_machine: Linear: plan → p-review → build → b-review → run → synthesize → s-review → complete. Reviews fail backward to prior action state. Reviews accumulate as REVIEW_<stage>_<n>.md. State stored in subprojects/<name>/kbu-subproject.toml. Hard-coded transition table in src/kbutillib/cli/subproject.py.
decisions.skill_authoring: Lean forks for kbu-plan (from /ai-design) / kbu-build (from /ai-conductor) / kbu-diagnose (from /diagnose). Harvested from BERIL for kbu-synthesize / kbu-review / kbu-literature-review. Net-new for kbu-run / kbu-update / both kbu-start files. Each lean-fork carries header comment: source skill + source commit + last review date. Independent evolution after fork.
decisions.manifest_format: TOML for both kbu-project.toml (root) and kbu-subproject.toml (subproject).
decisions.session_routing: kbu session save detects ~/Dropbox/Projects/AIAssistant/state/sessions.db (or ~/Projects/AIAssistant/state/sessions.db); routes to assistant.state.save_session with project_id=kbu-<repo>-<subproject>, auto-registering on first call. Else writes YAML to subprojects/<name>/sessions/<timestamp>-<skill>.yaml. Dashboard listing uses same detection.
decisions.venv_model: Two venvs. kbu init creates KBUtilLib venv (venvman if present else .venv) with editable install. kbu new-project creates per-project venv with KBUtilLib editable-installed from the SAME source path (single source of truth). Both register Jupyter kernels.
decisions.subproject_layout: subprojects/<name>/{kbu-subproject.toml, RESEARCH_PLAN.md, REPORT.md, REVIEW_<stage>_<n>.md, notebooks/{01_*.ipynb,util.py,nboutput/}, data/, user_data/, figures/, references.md, sessions/}. No root-level util.py or nboutput under notebooks/. jupyter-dev skill updated to mandate this even with one subproject.
decisions.update_model: Pull-update from parent KBUtilLib install path stored in kbu-project.toml [kbutillib].source_path. Clobber-with-warn: hash template files at last-pull, detect local modifications, prompt before overwrite. --set-source <path> for relocated repos. --from-github fallback deferred to v2.
decisions.cursor_strategy: Cursor workspace file generated by kbu new-project. cursor-setup skill (already in KBUtilLib) called by kbu init for detection. Do NOT auto-open Claude panel; print instructions to student instead.
decisions.harvest_decision: From BERIL-research-observatory (kbaseincubator/BERIL-research-observatory): adopt synthesize, berdl-review (as kbu-review stage-aware), literature-review. Reference berdl_start as design exemplar but don't copy. Drop pitfall-capture (deferred). Drop all BERDL-specific skills (berdl, berdl-ingest, berdl-query, minio, phenix, linkml, remote-compute, submit).
decisions.ai_assistant_integration: Tier-2 skills never import assistant.state for anything other than session routing. Session routing is opt-in via filesystem detection; absent AIAssistant the skill is fully self-contained.

modules.kbu_cli_subproject: kbu subproject {create,list,status,advance,set-status}. Deep: small surface, state-machine + manifest TOML I/O + precondition validation behind it. Tested.
modules.kbu_cli_session: kbu session {save,list,show}. Deep: AIAssistant detection + dual-mode storage (YAML local vs. SQLite via assistant.state) hidden behind small surface. Tested.
modules.kbu_cli_notebook: kbu notebook {list,mark-run,exec}. Medium: filesystem scan, manifest update, nbclient wrapper.
modules.kbu_cli_init: kbu init [--status|--update]. Deep: venvman/venv duality, idempotency via init-marker, jupyter kernel registration, doctor checks.
modules.kbu_cli_new_project: kbu new-project <path>. Deep: orchestration of template copy + child venv + editable install + git init + first-subproject scaffold.
modules.kbu_cli_update: kbu update [--set-source|--check]. Deep: parent git pull + template diff + locally-modified detection (hash comparison) + clobber-with-warn apply.
modules.tier1_kbu_start: Markdown skill at KBUtilLib/.claude/commands/kbu-start.md. Help / Initialize / New project / Update dashboard.
modules.tier2_kbu_start: Markdown skill at templates/student-project/.claude/commands/kbu-start.md. Status-aware dashboard with 8 menu items; surfaces kbu subproject list + kbu session list.
modules.kbu_plan_skill: Lean fork of /ai-design. Writes RESEARCH_PLAN.md, calls kbu subproject advance (plan→p-review) + session save.
modules.kbu_build_skill: Lean fork of /ai-conductor. Scaffolds notebooks per plan. Calls kbu subproject advance (build→b-review) + session save.
modules.kbu_run_skill: Net-new deliberate notebook dashboard. kbu notebook list → menu → kbu notebook exec <path>. Marks run, advances state on all-notebooks-complete, session save.
modules.kbu_synthesize_skill: Harvested from BERIL /synthesize. Two-pass findings → REPORT.md. Calls kbu subproject advance (synthesize→s-review) + session save.
modules.kbu_review_skill: Harvested from BERIL /berdl-review. Stage-aware (p-review/b-review/s-review). Writes REVIEW_<stage>_<n>.md. Calls kbu subproject advance (or --reverse on fail) + session save.
modules.kbu_literature_review_skill: Harvested from BERIL /literature-review. PubMed/bioRxiv/arXiv/Semantic Scholar search; appends references.md. Session save.
modules.kbu_diagnose_skill: Lean fork of /diagnose. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix. Session save.
modules.kbu_update_skill: Net-new skill wrapping kbu update. Shows diff, confirms, applies, reports.
modules.jupyter_dev_update: Modify KBUtilLib/agent-io/skills/jupyter-dev.md to mandate subproject layout unconditionally; reference kbu subproject create; remove permissive root-level guidance.

tests: state-machine transitions: forward + reverse + admin-override + precondition rejection
session routing detection: AIAssistant present vs absent; mock both paths
manifest TOML round-trip: full read + partial write append doesn't drop state
notebook list + modified-since-run flag: fixture mtimes vs recorded last_run_at
kbu new-project: template substitution, venv created, editable install, kbu-project.toml correct
kbu update clobber-with-warn: hash mismatch detected, prompt required, --force bypass, post-apply hash recorded
kbu update --set-source: relocates path, clears last_pulled_commit, next update re-evaluates
out_of_scope: pitfall-capture cross-cutting skill (BERIL has it; deferred for v1)
template_version migration support (defer to first breaking template change)
kbu update --from-github fallback (deferred to v2; --set-source covers moved repos)
Vendoring KBUtilLib into student projects (always editable-installed)
Cursor auto-opening the Claude panel (instructions only)
Auto-running notebooks in Plan or Build (only Run executes cells)
Tier-2 skills importing assistant.state for anything other than session routing
Backporting state machine to AIAssistant PRD lifecycle
kbu-start as a sub-agent in .claude/agents/ (slash command only)
Per-cell narration depth tuning (sensible default, iterate via feedback)
Multi-user collaboration on a single student project

dropped_reason.1: skill files don't exist — that's the implementation work the conductor will do
dropped_reason.2: template tree doesn't exist — same as #1, implementation work
dropped_reason.3: CLI subcommands don't exist — same, implementation work
dropped_reason.12: tests skeleton — existing tests/ conventions cover this; conductor follows established pattern
dropped_reason.13: skill markdown / AskUserQuestion format examples — slash-command format readable from existing skills in repo

confront_history.maestro_status: failed (FALSE NEGATIVE: codex completion sentinel; artifact committed + verified via git on task branch)
confront_history.advisory_critique_summary: Slice v1 to Initialize+New project+state machine first; kbu subproject validate command; template_version capture; safer notebook exec defaults; KBU_AIA_PATHS env var; Windows examples; golden fixtures; kbu doctor early; fork-header CI lint
